We need to determine the falling distance using d = k*t^2. Let me find k from the examples.
I will put my final answer inside \boxed{}.

t = 4.52s, d = 158.51m:
t^2 = 4.52 * 4.52:
4.52 * 0.02 = 0.0904
4.52 * 0.50 = 2.2600
4.52 * 4.00 = 18.0800
0.0904 + 2.2600 = 2.3504
2.3504 + 18.0800 = 20.4304
k = 158.51 / 4.52^2 = 158.51 / 20.4304 = 158.510 / 20.430
= 0 + 1 * 158510 / 20430
= 1 + 1 * 138080 / 20430
= 2 + 1 * 117650 / 20430
= 3 + 1 * 97220 / 20430
= 4 + 1 * 76790 / 20430
= 5 + 1 * 56360 / 20430
= 6 + 1 * 35930 / 20430
= 7 + 1 * 15500 / 20430
= 7.0 + 0.1 * 155000 / 20430
= 7.1 + 0.1 * 134570 / 20430
= 7.2 + 0.1 * 114140 / 20430
= 7.3 + 0.1 * 93710 / 20430
= 7.4 + 0.1 * 73280 / 20430
= 7.5 + 0.1 * 52850 / 20430
= 7.6 + 0.1 * 32420 / 20430
= 7.7 + 0.1 * 11990 / 20430
= 7.70 + 0.01 * 119900 / 20430
= 7.71 + 0.01 * 99470 / 20430
= 7.72 + 0.01 * 79040 / 20430
= 7.73 + 0.01 * 58610 / 20430
= 7.74 + 0.01 * 38180 / 20430
= 7.75 + 0.01 * 17750 / 20430
= 7.750 + 0.001 * 177500 / 20430
= 7.751 + 0.001 * 157070 / 20430
= 7.752 + 0.001 * 136640 / 20430
= 7.753 + 0.001 * 116210 / 20430
= 7.754 + 0.001 * 95780 / 20430
= 7.755 + 0.001 * 75350 / 20430
= 7.756 + 0.001 * 54920 / 20430
= 7.757 + 0.001 * 34490 / 20430
= 7.758 + 0.001 * 14060 / 20430
= 7.758

For t = 4.21:
t^2 = 4.21 * 4.21:
4.21 * 0.01 = 0.0421
4.21 * 0.20 = 0.8420
4.21 * 4.00 = 16.8400
0.0421 + 0.8420 = 0.8841
0.8841 + 16.8400 = 17.7241
= 17.7241

d = 7.758 * 17.7241:
7.758 * 0.0001 = 0.0007758
7.758 * 0.0040 = 0.0310320
7.758 * 0.0200 = 0.1551600
7.758 * 0.7000 = 5.4306000
7.758 * 7.0000 = 54.3060000
7.758 * 10.0000 = 77.5800000
0.0007758 + 0.0310320 = 0.0318078
0.0318078 + 0.1551600 = 0.1869678
0.1869678 + 5.4306000 = 5.6175678
5.6175678 + 54.3060000 = 59.9235678
59.9235678 + 77.5800000 = 137.5035678
= 137.503

I will now return the answer in \boxed{}
The answer in \boxed{–} is \boxed{137.503}
